name: Sauteed Tempeh with Lemon-Mustard Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the first eight ingredients in a large stock pot. Bring to a boil and then lower the heat. Simmer for two hours.
2. Strain the stock and discard the waste.
3. Combine 3-1/2 cups of the stock with the tamari or soy sauce. Bring the mixture to a simmer and steam the tempeh in the stock for 20 minutes. Remove the tempeh and allow it to cool.
4. Slice the tempeh into thin, long strips and dust it with flour.
5. Heat the oil in a medium frying pan over medium heat. Add the mushrooms and tempeh and saute until the tempeh is golden and the mushrooms are well cooked, about 10 to 15 minutes. Remove from the pan.
6. Keep the pan hot and add 1/2 cup of vegetable stock and the lemon juice. Then add the mustard and chives. Simmer, uncovered, for one minute.
7. Put the tempeh and mushrooms in a serving dish and cover with the lemon-mustard sauce. Serve immediately.
Serves 2 to 4
Preparation Time: 1 hour
Cooking Time: 2 hours
